content: The hierarchical, error-correcting structure of agent self-identity can be framed as an implicit intrinsic reward signal for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning, where mismatches between the agent's current self-model and observed interactions with the environment work exactly like prediction error in ICM or RND to drive exploratory behavior. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where curious exploration not only solves extrinsic tasks but also gradually refines the agent's own self-representation over its lifespan.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
